observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the concerned responsible personnel to submit the complete year-end financial statements, trial balances, adjusting and closing journal entry vouchers, subsidiary schedules, and bank reconciliation statements, otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned personnel pursuant to Section 41 (3) of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the amended Annual Investment Plan or supplemental Investment Program and ensure that the rules and regulations on 20% Development Fund utilization are appropriately followed; and
Carry out the priority projects as planned and as scheduled so that the Municipality and its constituents can immediately obtain the benefits from it, rather than realigning the project, to avoid waste of time, money, and efforts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Exhaust all possible remedies to collect and demand liquidation of cash advances from accountable officers, otherwise request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.
b) Follow up the COA-ICFAU as to the status of liquidation of cash advance from confidential fund.
c) Stop immediately the granting of additional cash advances to accountable officers with unliquidated balance.

We recommended that Management:
a) Discontinue the recording of inventory items as an outright expense and observe the proper recording of inventories as prescribed under the MNGAS for LGUs.
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Accounting Office to provide as basis in recording the corresponding expense accounts for the issuance.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to properly identify the PPE items and indicate all the needed details of the RPCPPE.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 162 of COA Circular 92 - 386.
Lastly, direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up the allowance for impairment consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 21, to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the SL for GF and SEF to facilitate the reconciliation of the balances thereof, in compliance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
b) Revert the unutilized balance of the LGSF to the National Treasury in compliance with Local Budget Circular No. 122.

We recommended that the Management:
a) Submit the lacking documentary requirements and ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation as prescribed in COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2009-001 and comply with the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.
b) Henceforth, the BAC conduct the post-qualification evaluation thoroughly, and ensure that all submitted eligibility requirements and statements are verified and checked in compliance with the procedures set forth under the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the lacking Mayor's permit and PhilGeps Registration Number, and henceforth, see to it that the required documents as enumerated in Appendix A of Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 are complied with prior to the award of the contract.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to properly plan the procurement of goods and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 particularly on the use of the alternative modes of procurements to promote transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.
Also, we recommended that Management withhold the prescribed taxes in all of its procurement of goods or services in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission of contracts and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five working days from the execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No, 2009-001.
We further recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of Section 447 of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure that the detailed engineering services are done in accordance with acceptable standards and practices.

We recommended that the Management distribute the shares of real property tax due to the barangays within five days after the end of each quarter and refrain from subjecting the shares to any deductions unless duly warranted by applicable laws or regulations pursuant to Section 271 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Development Council, submit an explanation/ justification for the delayed and non-implementation of the priority projects for CYs 2013-2021.
We also recommended that Management to always ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions outlined in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Prepare the list of all on-going Government Projects/ Programs/ Activities at the beginning of the year in compliance with the COA Circular No. 2013-004 to avoid imposition of any penalties/ sanctions related thereto.
b) Submit to the Audit Team a notification within ten days after the award of an infrastructure project that appropriate signboards and/or public notices have already been posted.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents for evaluation by the Audit Team and ensure that the prescribed documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 are complete and updated prior to the processing of monetization of the unused leave credits.

We recommended that Management avail/use the free computerized accounting and budgetary systems provided by the Commission on Audit by complying with the checklist of requirements for the installation.
Otherwise, we recommended that the Municipality substantiate its continued use of a manual system and the privately developed accounting system software, which may adversely affect the promotion of a transparent, efficient, and effective operation in government.

We recommended that Management:
a) Submit to the Audit Team the distribution list duly acknowledged by the beneficiaries pertaining to the release of the food items.
b) Discontinue the recording of inventory items as an outright expense and observe the proper recording of inventories as prescribed under the MNGAS for LGUs.
c) Henceforth, comply with the provisions of NDRRMC DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and ensure that LDRRM funds are spent in accordance with the rules and regulations intended for the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute the GAD Focal Point System to accelerate gender mainstreaming within the Municipality in accordance with the structure and composition provided for in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013- 01.

We required the immediate and timely submission of the October to December 2020 disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for proper accountability. Otherwise, the Municipal Mayor should suspend the payment of salaries of concerned officials pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD 1445.

Strict compliance with Section 100 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Government Accounting Manual is hereby recommended.

procedures and consequently strengthen or institute the appropriate control
mechanisms to ensure that prescribed rules and procedures judiciously and
faithfully adhered to in the disbursement of public funds. Concerned employees
should likewise undergo the necessary trainings and seminars for an efficient and
effective government operations.

We recommended that the municipal mayor issue an executive order
institutionalizing the establishment of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System in
accordance with the structure and composition provided for in PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer and the GFPS to lead or spearhead in the
setting up and maintenance of a GAD database to serve as basis for gender-
responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in compliance with the
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management establish a special account under the
General Fund for the operation of its Public Utilities and Economic Enterprises
in accordance withSection 313 of R.A. 7160and DBM Local Budget Circular No.
111.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor provide the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with an
updated RPTAR showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible as basis for setting up RPT/SET receivables in the books of accounts in
conformity with NGAS Manual and pertinent provisions of PPSAS.
We also recommendedthat theMunicipalAccountant make the necessary
adjustments so that the RPT receivables and SET receivables and their
corresponding accounts can be properly presented in the financial statements. We
further recommend that subsidiary ledgers for the taxpayers be properly
maintain to facilitate monitoring and proper handling of accounts.

We recommended that management immediately correct the erroneous
computation of GSIS personal share in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No.
8291; and conduct reconciliation on the noted under-remittances to ensure that
that what is deducted from the employees’ salaries is remitted intactin accordance
with P.D. No. 1445.

We recommend for the immediate submission of the Financial
Statements required under COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2,
2010 and Section 122 of the P.D. 1445.

We further recommend that continued failure on the part of the
officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically
cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have
complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2,
Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that Management submit the justification for the
deficiency of the transaction, otherwise, persons responsible for the
inordinate proceeding should be held accountable pursuant to the
penalties and administrative sanctions provided for under RA 9184 and
its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management exert due
diligence in the conduct of its procurement proceedings and ensure that
provisions of the procurement are strictly adhered to avoid personal
liability.

We recommend that Management submit the bidding and
eligibility documents as well as the duly verified inspection and
accomplishment reports for evaluation and other pertinent documentary
requirements pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 for procurement
involving public bidding. Failure of the Management to adhere shall be
a ground for issuance of notice of suspension.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant refrain from
processing payments with incomplete documentary requirements to
avoid personal liability which is contrary to sound internal accounting
system.

We recommend that Management immediately prepare and
submit the financial reports and books of accounts for the
implementation of the KfW-YRP project for evaluation by this office, as
well as the pertinent supporting documents on the fund transfers made.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management maintain records to
reflect the financial result of the program. Failure of management to submit
the same shall be a ground for the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee through the
BAC Secretariat provide justification for the absence of invitation to
independent observers as required under RA 9184. Moreover, ensure
that notices or invitations are served to the minimum required
independent observers with appropriate proof of receipt pursuant to
Section 13 of RA 9184.

We recommend that the BAC submit the complete bidding
documents and the eligibility requirements of the above-cited
transaction for evaluation by this office to avoid eventual suspension of
the transactions.

We also recommend that henceforth, the BAC and Management
award procurement contracts only to qualified suppliers and ensure that
all pertinent documentary requirements relative to the municipality’s
procurements are complete and adequately made available.

We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) duly executed by and between the Municipality of
Madridejos, Cebu and the DILG, DSWD, DOLE and LGSF and Special
Allotment Release Order (SARO) to determine the standards of
implementation of the different projects and whether the BuB projects
were implemented in accordance with such standards.

We recommend that Management submit justification for the non

disqualification of the winning bidders who failed to post the
Performance Security within the reglementary period, otherwise, the
person responsible for the transactions should be held accountable
pursuant to the penalties and sanctions provided in RA 9184 and its
revised IRR.

We recommend that the General Services Office conduct a
complete physical inventory count of properties, prepare and submit to
the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the
properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section
124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We also recommend that management assign permanent
personnel to give priority in the reconciliation of the records pursuant to
Section 114 of NGAS Manual, Volume I, otherwise continued failure on
the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall
automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until
they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by
virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) duly executed by and between the Municipality of
Madridejos, Cebu and CHED to determine the standards of
implementation of the program and if it was implemented in accordance
with such standards.

Likewise, we recommend that the Special Disbursing Officer
submit liquidation reports including the corresponding utilization
reports, master list and payrolls for evaluation. Otherwise, a notice of
suspension will be issued to this effect.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management ensure that
receipt and disbursement of government funds be supported with the
necessary and pertinent documents in adherence to COA Circular Nos.
94-013 and 2012-001 for proper disposition of funds.

We recommend that Management submit the reviewed and
approved DILG GAD Plan and Budget. We also recommend that
Management ensures that GAD Fund is utilized in accordance with the
Joint Circular PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and avoid unnecessary expenses
which do not address gender issues.

We recommend that Management submit justification for the non-
disqualification of the winning bidder who failed to post the
Performance Security, otherwise, persons responsible for the inordinate
proceeding should be held accountable pursuant to the penalties and
administrative sanctions provided for under RA 9184 and its
Implementing Rules and Regulations.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management exert due
diligence in the conduct of its procurement proceedings and ensure that
provisions of the procurement are strictly adhered to avoid personal
liability.

We recommend that the following remedial measures be
undertaken by Management:
1. Review the programs under its Solid Waste Management and
20% Development Fund and refrain from charging ineligible
items that do not fall under the guidelines set forth under the
DILG and DBM JCM No. 2017-1 and provisions of RA 9003.
2. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to explain the
aforementioned irregularity and submit the duly approved
Supplemental Budget or Realignment Resolutions pertinent to
the excess disbursement, otherwise a notice of suspension will
be issued to this effect.
3. Prohibit from obligating or incurring expenditures which were
not supported with a duly approved appropriation, or without
first securing an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan thru a
supplemental budget appropriation as the case may be to avoid
personal liability.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer immediately demand
from all liable persons to settle the above disallowance or withhold the
salaries of liable persons still in service, otherwise, refusal to comply
with the order or decision of the Commission shall constitute a ground
for contempt and/or referral of the matter to the Solicitor General for the
filing of appropriate civil suit or to the Ombudsman for the filing of
appropriate administrative or criminal action against the officer or
employee concerned as warranted, pursuant to the provisions set forth
in the revised rules and procedures of the Commission on Audit.

We recommend that the SB Secretary submit an explanation as to
the issuance of the abovementioned resolutions, despite the non-
approval thereof and non-inclusion of same in the minutes of the SB
session. Likewise, direct responsible official to submit a justification for
the issuance of the checks despite the non-approval of the Municipal
Mayor and the incomplete documentation, and to submit authenticated
copies of the SB resolution, related minutes and pertinent documentary
requirements for the above transactions. Failure of Management to
submit the same will be a ground for the issuance of notice of
suspension.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management exercise due
diligence in the preparation and issuance of public documents and
ensure that disbursements for financial subsidy be supported with the
documentary requirements in accordance with PD 1445 and COA
Circular 2012-001, and ensure that prior subsidies were fully accounted
for monitoring purposes and the proper disposition of government
funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Local School Board submit a
justification for the non-preparation of the SEF budget for the CY 2017,
otherwise, persons responsible for the SEF disbursements sans the
duly approved appropriation will be held liable. Henceforth, the
utilization of the Municipality’s SEF should adhere to the provisions
Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommend that Management refrain from indicating in the
Request for Quotation the specific brand name when purchasing
equipment and other items. Likewise, strict compliance of Section 18 of
RA 9184 should be observed.

We recommend that Management request a notarized certification
from Chiong Gen. Merchandise affirming the authenticity of the
submitted RFQ or require the same to countersign the noted alteration
to reflect the supplier’s approval thereof, otherwise the Bids and Award
Committee or the parties responsible must submit an explanation on the
inordinate nature of the transaction without prejudice to the
administrative penalties and sanction set forth in RA 9184. Failure of
Management to submit the required documents will be a ground for the
issuance of notice of suspension.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management ensure that
documents pertaining to any government transaction must be duly and
faithfully evaluated and verified to assure authenticity thereof and
prohibit the processing of payments for transactions with unjustified
erasures or alterations and incomplete documentary requirements to
avoid personal liability.

We recommend that Management recover the excess amount paid
to the supplier, otherwise accountable officials will be held liable for the
irregular expenditure. A notice of disallowance will be issued to this
effect.

Henceforth, we recommend that Management ensure that
documents for money claims against the government are complete, duly
reviewed and validated to avoid incurring irregular payments, without
prejudice to the applicable penalties and sanctions under the applicable
laws.

We recommend that Management execute a yearly contract of
lease embodied in a public instrument for every public market stall
occupant outlining the terms and conditions as well as effectivity
thereof to ensure that the interests of both parties are protected
pursuant to COA Circular No. 88-282A.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor consider that the hiring
of job order personnel be based on necessity and should be
identified/attributed to specific projects for a period not exceeding six
months pursuant to Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules on
Appointments and Other Personnel Actions, under CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 40 and Section 77 of R.A. 7160.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management through the
Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) evaluate the
Municipality’s manpower structure and present system on the hiring of
its Job Order workers and consequently establish appropriate
guidelines or policies to ensure that hiring are in accordance with the
agency’s needs, service requirements, financial capabilities and of the
workers capabilities and qualifications pursuant to provisions set forth
by government regulating bodies with a view of ensuring efficiency,
economy and effectiveness in its operation and the disposition of
government’s scarce resources. Likewise, maintain adequate personnel
records to facilitate easy verification and monitoring of the agency’s
manpower resources.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor direct the responsible
officials to reconcile and transfer the remaining unexpended balance
from General Fund to Trust Fund in compliance with the provisions of
RA No. 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 for proper monitoring of the
utilization of the Local DRRM Fund and utilize the funds in accordance
with COA Circular 2012-002 and applicable laws.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management closely monitor
the implementation of programs/projects/activities of LDRRM Fund such
that the objectives for the provision of this fund may be met.

We recommend that the current Municipal School Board revisit
the provisions of the Section 98 (b) of R.A. 7160 and include the
representative of the pederasyon ng mga Sangguniang Kabataan in the
Sangguniang Bayan, the duly elected representative of the teachers’
organizations in the municipality, and the duly elected representative of
the non-academic personnel of public schools, as members.

We recommend for an immediate submission of the revised and complete FS and Trial Balance pursuant to Section 41(2) of P.D. 1445.

We further recommend that continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Section 41(3) of P.D. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for an immediate submission of the revised and
complete FS and Trial Balance pursuant to Section 41(2) of P.D. 1445.

We further recommend that continued failure on the part of the
officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically
cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have
complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Section
41(3) of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) duly executed by and between the Municipality of
Madridejos, Cebu and the DA, DILG, DOH, DSWD, DOLE and LGSF and
Special Allotment Release Order (SARO) to determine the standards of
implementation of the different projects and whether the BuB projects
were implemented in accordance with such standards.

We recommend that Management immediately reconcile the
material variances between the records and reports and general and
subsidiary ledgers. Carry forward previous years’ balances of officers and
employees to the current year and that the amount forwarded should be equal.

We also recommend that Management conduct trainings and re-orientation of
accounting staff regarding the basic process, established standards, rules and
regulations of the accounting system in the public sector.

We recommend that the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer
conduct a complete physical count of properties and submit to the Auditor an
updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than
January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual,
Volume I.

We also recommend that Management assign permanent
personnel to give priority in the reconciliation of the records pursuant to
Section 114 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
Secretariat submit the Performance Security of the aforementioned
projects as well as the Duly Approved Contract and Post-Qualification
Report of the Technical Working Group.

We recommend that, henceforth, the BAC exercise due diligence
in the conduct of post-qualification process and if warranted, disqualify
any bidder identified as the LCRB, who fails to post the required
performance security within the period prescribed. Also, ensure that
notices issued to the contractors are duly received pursuant to the
provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations is
enjoined.

We recommend that Management thoroughly evaluate any
appropriations intended for the loan repayment to ensure that budget
allocations be made only to those approved loan applications whose
availments are probable or whose claims are duly established and the
status of any loan or timeline for any loan application should also be
monitored so that allocations will be utilized for other priority projects
before the end of each calendar year.

We recommend that Management ensure that the 20%
development fund is optimally utilized to benefit the Municipality’s
constituencies and that each project or program should indicate the
target implementation and completion schedules for evaluation and
monitoring purposes.

We recommend that Management adhere to the documentary
requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 for procurement
involving public bidding and prohibit processing of payment requests
for mobilization and/or progress billings with incomplete supporting
documents. Failure of the Management to adhere shall be a ground for
issuance of notice of suspension.

We recommend that Management ascertain the unexpended
balances of the LDRRMF for CY 2012-2014 and immediately transfer the
same, including that of calendar years 2015 and 2016 to a special trust
fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and
management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five (5) years.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management observe the
accounting rules on the recording of said fund as well as ensure that
reportorial requirements and other procedural rules are adhered as set
forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions
of RA 9184 and refrain from immediately resorting to shopping without
any justification, action or recommendation from the Bids and Awards
Committee and that price quotations from at least three (3) bona fide
suppliers should be duly and faithfully accomplished. Furthermore,
BAC resolution and Abstract of Canvass should thereafter be attached
as part of the supporting documents for every procurement. Failure of
management to observe the same may be a ground for issuance of
notice of suspension and eventual disallowance.

We recommend that henceforth, the Local School Board must
prepare an Annual Budget in accordance with the rules and regulations
of Section 99 of RA 7160.

We further recommend that the Local School Board see to it that
Special Education Fund be utilized for purposes provided under the
above-cited regulations to ensure that the purpose for which the fund
was established is met.

We recommend that Management submit the certified copies of
the transmittal letters of the liquidations duly received by the COA
Chairman or authorized representative or official receipt if sent through
registered mail/courier.. Otherwise, the responsible officer should be
held accountable for violation of COA Circular No. 2003-003.

We also recommend that the accountable officer submit the
required supporting documents for confidential funds released in 2015.
Henceforth, cash advances for confidential funds should be supported
with complete documentation and be submitted in accordance to the
provisions set forth in of COA DBM DILG GCG DND Joint Circular No.
2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
strictly adhere the provisions of RA 9184 and its implementing rules and
regulations. Moreover, the members of the BAC are required to justify
their failure to conduct a second bidding on the procurement of
materials, supply of labor and rental of equipment on the Road
Concreting Project of Villacarlos Street-Phase II before resorting to
negotiated procurement.

We also recommend that Management submit the Minutes of
Meeting and BAC Resolution dated December 3 and 11, 2014,
respectively, duly signed by the BAC Chairman. Failure on their part to
submit the said documents shall be a ground for the issuance of notice
of disallowance.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer undertake immediate
repairs on the noted deficiencies of the project.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
justify the inconsistencies in the dates of the Request for Quotation,
Delivery Receipts and Purchase Request. In addition, we also
recommend that Management should be cautious in the preparation of
documents and in the certifications thereof. As public officials, they are
expected to exercise the highest degree of diligence in the performance
of their mandated duties and this include the preparation and
certification of documents to support disbursements of public funds.

We recommend that the necessary Programs of Work be prepared
before any procurement of infrastructure projects or repair or
rehabilitation of infrastructure projects as required under the Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act 9184 specifically No. 3 of Annex A in order
to facilitate inspection and evaluation of the project implemented.
Furthermore, we recommend that before and after any repair of
Municipal Vehicles and Equipment, actual inspection and preparation of
the pertinent reports should both be made.

We recommend that Management thru the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) justify the use of alternative methods of procurement
including the conditions to warrant the same and submit to this office
for evaluation.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management refrain from
splitting purchases to circumvent procedural purchase limits and to
adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations.

We recommend that Management subject the financial assistance
to Barangays to regular liquidation and require submission of utilization
reports for proper disposition and monitoring.

We also recommend that each request for financial assistance out
of the lumpsum appropriation be supported with Memorandum of
Agreement and authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and that
disbursement vouchers should bear a certification from the Municipal
Accountant that the previous transferred funds to the LGU-Barangays
had been liquidated, and accounted for in the books of account.

We recommend that Management refrain from violating Section
89 of RA 7160 to prevent the imposition of sanctions provided for by
law.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and
submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement as required under
the applicable provisions of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and concerned
accountable officers prepare and submit the Monthly Consolidated
Report of Accountability of Accountable Forms (CRAAF) within the
prescribed period for verification.

We recommend that Management immediately set up and
recognize the receivable account arising from the Notices of Finality of
Decision pursuant to Sections 7.2.1 and 22.6 of COA Circular No. 2009-
006 and credit the expenses related thereto. In addition, we also
recommend that Management direct the persons liable to pay or refund
the amount disallowed.

We too recommend that Management adhere to the COA
Resolution No. 2015-031 in the settlement of Notices of Disallowance.

We recommend that Management ensure that purchase orders
invariably bear the basic and vital data pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-
010 and that basic documentary requirements are faithfully and duly
accomplished.

We recommend that the fidelity bonds of the accountable officers
be immediately renewed pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009
dated August 6, 2009, otherwise, said accountable officers should
desist from performing their functions until renewal of fidelity bond is
secured.
